ead.archdesc:
======= RMA03591Stephen Maxfield Parrish papers14-12-3591Compiled by Meaghan CorbettDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Stephen Maxfield Parrish papers
Parrish, Stephen Maxfield.
Curtis, Jared R., 1936-
Butler, James, 1945-
Jaye, Michael C., 1941-
Bushell, Sally.
Wordsworth, J. W. (Jonathan W.)
Wordsworth, Richard.
Reed, Mark L.
Saddlemyer, Ann.
14-12-3591
15.6 cubic feet. 15.6 cubic feet.
1901-2010. Printed Materials, Correspondence, Research Materials, Microfilm, Photographs Includes correspondence, book reviews, subject files, and course material relating to his study of William Wordsworth and his career at Cornell. Also, correspondence between Jared Curtis, Coordinating Editor, and Stephen M. Parrish concerning The Cornell Wordsworth series and The Cornell Yeats series, as well as correspondence from others involved in those editing projects. Collection material in English 6049900
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Meaghan Corbett
Date completed:
July 16, 2007
EAD encoding:
Meaghan Corbett, 2007
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of English, Cornell University.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence
Series II. Publications
Series III. Freud
Series IV. Manuscript Materials
Series V. Various Material
Series VI: Audio/visual media
General
CD-1594 through CD-1610 contains manuscript materials, including transcriptions and photographic reproductions of interim stages of editions from Jared Curtis - Cornell Yeats series
Cite As:
Stephen Maxfield Parrish papers, #14/12/3591. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Includes correspondence, book reviews, and manuscript files relating to his study of William Wordsworth, Sigmund Freud, and William Yeats and material documenting his career at Cornell.
Scope and content
Also, correspondence between Jared Curtis, Coordinating Editor, and Stephen M. Parrish concerning The Cornell Wordsworth series and The Cornell Yeats series, as well as correspondence from others involved in those editing projects.
Scope and content
Correspondence of James Butler with Stephen M. Parrish, 1972-1996 relating to the publication of the Lyrical Ballads volume in the Wordsworth series and to the series in general. Also, correspondence, 1974-2005, of Michael Jaye and James Butler with Stephen M. Parrish about the Wordsworth series edition of The Excursion, published in 2007.
Scope and content
Photographs by James Butler of an event celebrating the completion of the Wordsworth series held at Kendal at Ithaca on March 29, 2008.
Scope and content
Correspondence with Sally Bushell relating to The Excursion,1999-2002.
Scope and content
Correspondence of Mark L. Reed with Parrish, and letters between other editors and scholars relating to the series. Includes additional correspondence between Reed and Curtis, James Averill, Stephen Gill, Carl Ketcham, and Robert Osborn.
Scope and content
Cassette tapes of the Wordsworth Summer Conferences, 1991: Jonathan Wordsworth on Wordsworthian Comedy and 1998: Stephen Parrish on The Earliest Wordsworth; "Richard Wordsworth Reads the Poetry," and Jonathan Wordsworth/Studs Terkel talk.
Scope and content
Correspondence, grant proposals, reviews, and other files from Ann Saddlemyer, one of the general editors, relating to The Cornell Yeats series. Includes 17 CDs of interim stages of editions from Jared Curtis.
English literature -- Study and teaching. College teachers -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Wordsworth, William, 1770-1850. Yeats, W. B. (William Butler), 1865-1939. Freud, Sigmund, 1856-1939. Cornell University. English Department Cornell University -- : Faculty.

Scope and Contents
"Diarmuid and Grania" – by Yeats and George Moore (n.d.), edited by J. C. C. Mays - intro
"The Only Jealousy of Emer" – untranscribed version
